title: Field-Conformity Rule Pressure
summary: FIA dry-race rules require using two dry compounds, but the current features mostly encode only the driver’s own stop/compound debt.
rationale: FIA dry-race rules require using two dry compounds, but the current features mostly encode only the driver’s own stop/compound debt. Strategy is also relative: pit windows, cover stops, and same-compound herd behavior are field-level phenomena.
implementation_hint: On dry slick laps, compute per race-lap fractions of drivers who still owe the mandatory compound, already made a first stop, are on the same compound with older tyres, or are pitting now. Interact these field-pressure features with the driver’s own prior pit count, prior slick count, position, and tyre finish-pressure features.
expected_effect: Adds race-state coordination and compliance pressure without needing full sequence models.
risk: Needs careful gating for wet/intermediate races and Monaco-specific rule differences.
